name: American Airlines
description_short: American Airlines Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering banking, lending, and credit card products exclusively to American Airlines employees and their families.
description_long:
American Airlines Federal Credit Union (AAFCU) is a federally chartered, not-for-profit credit union serving employees of American Airlines and their eligible family members. Established to provide financial services tailored to the airline industry workforce, AAFCU operates under NCUA insurance and maintains a routing number (311992904) alongside nationwide ATM access. The institution has celebrated its 90th anniversary as of 2026, indicating a long operational history within the employee credit union space.

AACFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products across banking, borrowing, and credit services. On the deposit side, members can access savings accounts (up to 1.87% APY), share certificates with rates up to 4.07% APY on ladder certificates, and checking accounts (Priority Checking at 0.40% APY and Flagship Checking at 0.25% APY). For borrowing, the credit union provides auto loans (3.74% APR for new autos up to 36 months), home mortgages (15-year fixed at 5.702% APR, 30-year fixed at 5.821% APR), and specialty vehicle loans for RVs and boats/airplanes. Credit card products include Signature Cash Back (14.25%-16.25% APR), Platinum Rewards (12.00%-17.74% APR), Platinum Low Rate (10.50%-18.00% APR), and Platinum Secured cards. Business banking services are available for small business members.

What distinguishes AAFCU is its focused membership model serving American Airlines personnel and families, offering travel-focused rewards products such as the Visa Platinum Rewards card with special 90th anniversary bonuses (up to $90 for qualifying new checking accounts). The institution emphasizes member benefits through competitive rates on savings and lending products, ATM access across a network, and online banking capabilities. The credit union structure aligns with member interests rather than shareholder profits, theoretically prioritizing member value.

However, membership is restricted to American Airlines employees and eligible family members, making this inaccessible to the general public. Credit card APR ranges are notably broad (particularly the Platinum Rewards at 12.00%-17.74%), suggesting rates vary significantly based on creditworthiness. Deposit rates, while competitive for certificates, are modest on checking and savings accounts compared to some online-only banks. Limited transparency exists on the website regarding loan approval requirements, membership eligibility specifics, or detailed fee schedules beyond rate cards.
services: Savings accounts (Share/IRA accounts up to 1.87% APY)
Share certificates and ladder certificates (up to 4.07% APY)
Checking accounts (Priority Checking and Flagship Checking)
Auto loans (new and used, up to 60 months)
Home mortgages (15-year fixed, 30-year fixed, 5/1 ARM, 5/10 fixed)
Home equity lines of credit (HELOC up to 7.250% APR)
Visa credit cards (Signature Cash Back, Platinum Rewards, Platinum Low Rate, Platinum Secured)
Online banking platform with login access
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 3.74% APR for new vehicles financed up to 36 months
Share certificate rates up to 4.07% APY on ladder certificates, competitive for credit union products
Visa Platinum Rewards card with travel-focused benefits and special 90th anniversary promotion (up to $90 cash bonus)
Mortgage rates as low as 5.702% APR on 15-year fixed and 5.821% on 30-year fixed loans
Comprehensive product suite including specialty vehicle loans (RVs, boats, airplanes) uncommon at many banks
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure aligns institutional interests with member financial well-being
NCUA insurance protection on deposits and nationwide ATM network access for members
cons: Membership restricted to American Airlines employees and eligible family members; inaccessible to general public
Credit card APR ranges are extremely broad (Platinum Rewards spans 12.00%-17.74%), leaving uncertainty on actual approval rates
Checking account APY rates very low (0.25%-0.40%), significantly underperforming online banks offering 4%+ on checking
Website provides limited detail on specific membership eligibility, loan approval criteria, or comprehensive fee schedules
Savings account APY of 1.87% lags behind competitive online-only savings institutions
best_for: American Airlines employees seeking specialized financial products with travel rewards and airline-focused benefits
Current and former American Airlines staff seeking competitive auto and home loan rates through a member-owned institution
Eligible family members of American Airlines employees looking for NCUA-insured deposit accounts and credit services
diagnosis: American Airlines Federal Credit Union is best for American Airlines employees and their eligible family members seeking competitive lending rates and member-focused banking services. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to AA staff and families, making this inaccessible to the general public despite offering quality credit union products.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: American Airlines — Fort Worth, TX Credit Union | CreditDoc
meta_description: American Airlines — NCUA-insured credit union in Fort Worth, TX. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
